type.description:
An open-ended discriminator — a server may register additional
types beyond these four (see `PostTypeHandler` in the developer
docs); treat an unrecognized value as a plain update. `content`
stays the universal human-readable field for every type (a
poll's question, for instance — a `TRIP` post's `content` is
plain free text, its own identity lives in `typeData`/`trip`
instead); type-specific configuration lives in `typeData`.

typeData.description:
Type-specific configuration, `null` for `UPDATE`/`MILESTONE`. For
`POLL`: `{ options: [{ id, text }], closesAt }` — the stored
options (immutable after creation) and optional ISO 8601 close
time. For `TRIP`: `{ title, destination, startDate, endDate, coverPhotoUrl, travelerUserIds, closedAt, closedByUserId }`
— `closedAt`/`closedByUserId` (both `null` until the trip is
closed) and `travelerUserIds` are the only parts of a trip's
`typeData` that change after creation, written by the `close`
and `setTravelers` interactions respectively; every other field
is immutable, same as a poll's options. Not present on the
response to `POST /api/posts` or `PATCH /api/posts/{id}` beyond
what you sent — read the poll's live vote tallies from `poll`,
or a trip's live aggregates from `trip`, below instead.

allOf.description:
The enriched, read-time view of a `POLL` post's `typeData` — computed
fresh on every read, never stored as-is. Options and vote counts are
visible to every group member whether or not they've voted; votes are
not anonymous (see `voters` on each option).

voters.description: Everyone who voted for this option, oldest vote first.

myVoteOptionId.description: The option id you voted for, or `null` if you haven't voted.

closed.description: `true` once `closesAt` has passed. Voting a closed poll is rejected.

allOf.title: PostPoll

poll.description:
Present only when `type` is `POLL` — the enriched, read-time view
(vote counts, voters, your own vote). Computed on every read, so
it's absent from `POST /api/posts` and `PATCH /api/posts/{id}`
responses (fetch the post again, e.g. via `GET /api/posts/{id}`,
to see it) — the same precedent as `people` below.

allOf.description:
The enriched, read-time view of a `TRIP` post's `typeData` — computed
fresh on every read, never stored as-is. A trip's check-ins are its
`Comment` rows with `metadata.kind: "trip_checkin"` (see `Comment`
below); this object aggregates them rather than requiring a second
fetch.

travelers.description:
Co-travelers who may also check in, resolved from
`typeData.travelerUserIds` — never includes the post's own
author, who is an implicit traveler shown separately via
`author`.

closed.description:
`true` once the trip was explicitly closed (`close` interaction)
or its `endDate`'s day has fully elapsed (auto-close) — either
way, `checkin`/`close`/`setTravelers` are then rejected.

closedAt.description: When the trip was explicitly closed; `null` if it's still open or only auto-closed by `endDate`.

dayNumber.description: Which day of the trip today is (1-based), or `null` once `closed`.

durationDays.description: Total trip length in days, or `null` while still open with no `endDate`.

photoCount.description: Total number of photos across every check-in.

collagePhotoUrls.description: Up to the 3 most recent check-in photos, newest first.

allOf.title: PostTrip

trip.description:
Present only when `type` is `TRIP` — the enriched, read-time view
of the trip (day counter, stop/photo counts, latest check-in,
a small photo collage, resolved travelers). Computed on every
read, so — like `poll` — it's absent from `POST /api/posts` and
`PATCH /api/posts/{id}` responses.

uploadedAssetUrls.description: `/uploads/...` or `/api/immich/assets/...` paths — auth-gated, see Uploads & media.

reactions.description: Per-emoji reaction counts; only emojis with at least one reaction appear.
reactions.title: ReactionCounts

recentReactors.description: The three most recent reactors, newest first.

items.description:
A person mapped by an admin and recognized in a post's photos.
If linked to a Famlin user, includes their profile information.

id.description: The unique identifier for this mapping.

provider.description: Which media source this person's face data comes from.

label.description: Human-readable name set by the admin.

userId.description: If linked to a Famlin user, their user id; otherwise `null`.

userName.description: If linked to a Famlin user, their display name; otherwise `null`.

userAvatarUrl.description: If linked to a Famlin user, their avatar URL; otherwise `null`.

items.title: PostPerson

people.description: Admin-mapped media people recognized in this post's photos, empty if none matched.

sharedWithGroups.description:
Present only in responses to the post's **author**, only when the
post was cross-posted (created with 2+ `groupIds`). Lists every
group the post was shared with, including this response's own
`group`. Other members never see this field or otherwise learn a
post was cross-posted — each group's copy looks like an ordinary,
single-group post to them.

400.description:
Invalid request body (see `details`), or a post-type-specific
problem: an unrecognized `type`, `typeData` that doesn't match
the type's own schema (or was sent for a type that doesn't accept
one), — for a poll — an empty question, or — for a trip —
`endDate` before `startDate`, or a `travelerUserIds` entry that
isn't a current member of every target group.

details.description: Zod field errors, present only for body-shape problems.

401.description: Missing, invalid, expired, or revoked credential

403.description:
Not a member of a target group, or a target group's
`allowedPostTypes` doesn't include the requested `type` (for a
cross-post, every target group must allow it).
